title: Accessible Modal with Focus Trap
description:
Build an **accessible modal dialog** that properly traps focus, manages focus restoration, and supports keyboard dismissal.

### Requirements

- Modal has `role="dialog"` and `aria-modal="true"`
- Modal has `aria-labelledby` pointing to its title element
- When modal opens, focus moves to the first focusable element inside
- Tab key cycles through focusable elements within the modal (focus trap)
- Shift+Tab wraps backwards through the modal
- Pressing Escape closes the modal
- Clicking the overlay (outside the modal content) closes the modal
- When the modal closes, focus returns to the element that opened it
- The modal renders a portal (use `ReactDOM.createPortal`)

### Test IDs

| Element | data-testid |
|---------|-------------|
| Open button | `open-modal` |
| Modal overlay | `modal-overlay` |
| Modal content | `modal-content` |
| Modal title | `modal-title` |
| Close button | `modal-close` |
| Confirm button | `modal-confirm` |
| Cancel button | `modal-cancel` |
constraints:
- Focus must not leave the modal while it's open
- Focus must return to the trigger element on close
- Must use role='dialog' and aria-modal='true'
- Must work with keyboard-only navigation
- Escape key must close the modal
hints: Query all focusable elements inside the modal: buttons, inputs, anchors, [tabindex]. Store as an array and manage first/last.
On Tab keydown at the last element, preventDefault and focus the first. On Shift+Tab at the first, focus the last.
Use useRef to store the element that triggered the modal, and call triggerRef.current?.focus() on close.
